critical.mandate: Story files must include YAML frontmatter with key, status, dependencies
Check web access availability if story references external APIs

step.title: Select Story
step.ask: Which story do you want to detail? (provide story key from sprint plan or epics)

step.title: Web Access Guard
step.check: Check if web tools available for API documentation lookup

step.title: Elaborate Story
step.action: Expand acceptance criteria with detailed test conditions
Add technical notes from architecture context

step.title: Definition of Done
step.action: Append DoD checklist: all AC met, tests pass, code reviewed, docs updated

step.title: Generate Output
step.template-output: Generate story file using story-template.md. The filename must include the story number and story key (e.g., S01-user-login.md). Fill all template sections: User Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks (linked to AC numbers), Dev Notes, Technical Notes, Dependencies, Test Scenarios, Project Structure Notes, References, Dev Agent Record, and Estimate.

step.title: Update Sprint Status
step.action: Find the story entry matching {story_key} and update its status to ready-for-dev
If sprint-status.yaml does not exist, create it with the story entry set to ready-for-dev
step.template-output: Update the sprint status file with the story status set to ready-for-dev.
